summary: Defined the bounded M6 Alpha system-complete contract as a planning-only task. The contract specifies start conditions, one accepted non-map-painting victory condition, failure conditions, evidence requirements, no-placeholder major-system expectations, M1-M5 mapping, and M6 DAG mapping while preserving Human Gates and DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE.

tests_run.result: Modified only allowed task/status/message files at the time of the check; untracked ui-samples/ was present and left untouched.
Task list shows M6-ALPHA-SYSTEM-CONTRACT-001 in REVIEW; M6-TASK-GRAPH-001 remains CLOSED and downstream M6 implementation tasks remain DRAFT.
No READY tasks printed while M6-ALPHA-SYSTEM-CONTRACT-001 is in REVIEW.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M6-ALPHA-SYSTEM-CONTRACT-001
tests_run.result: JSON shape ok: required 19 contract sections present; 8 player decisions each include advantage, cost, dependency, and counterplay fields; route_to systems_architect; Human Gate false; manual decision unchanged.

decisions: M6 Alpha victory is bounded to one accepted recognized-order condition, not map painting: a valid political arrangement, legitimacy or diplomatic recognition evidence, obligation/governance terms, and a stabilization check are required.
Every gameplay advantage in the contract has a named cost, dependency, and counterplay path.
Consequences are institutional and ledger-based; the contract rejects hidden global penalties, total-character-score design, and fixed culture/ethnicity buffs.
Parameters are recorded as starting hypotheses with metrics and validation methods, not final balance values.
Formal historical names, claims, symbols, language, art, music, or culture-specific assets are outside this contract and must route to historical_researcher before production acceptance.
Manual node battle remains DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE: only a post-1.0 non-blocking candidate note is allowed, with no M6/M7/M8 implementation, spike, research, validation, or exit-gate dependency.
risks: Systems_architect should verify that the M6 major-system expectations are strict enough to prevent placeholder UI while still not expanding into M7 content lock or M8 release hardening.

The contract names a narrow accepted victory condition; future implementation tasks must keep it minimal and avoid adding full diplomacy, full peace treaties, complex naval war, or release/commercial scope.
Untracked ui-samples/ existed before this writer work and was intentionally untouched.
requested_action: Review the M6 Alpha system-complete contract for scope, architecture boundaries, M1-M5/M6 DAG mapping, Human Gate preservation, DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE preservation, and no-placeholder major-system expectations.
